title: get() ignores dataCrc32c integrity field in GCP SM response
description: get() parses payload.data and decodes it but never reads payload.dataCrc32c. GCP SM returns this CRC32C checksum expressly for client-side integrity verification. A storage corruption at GCP or a response truncation returns corrupt secret bytes with no error. TLS prevents MITM so the risk is low but non-zero; a secrets backend should use the integrity field GCP provides. Fix: compute crc32c of the decoded bytes and compare against dataCrc32c from the response, returning SecretError::DecodeFailed on mismatch. Requires adding a crc32c dependency (crc32fast or similar).
